value.found_quote: Tool-name globs are supported in deny rules: `"*"` matches every tool and `"mcp__*"` matches every MCP tool across all servers. Allow rules accept tool-name globs only after a literal `mcp__<server>__` prefix. The server segment must be glob-free so the rule names a specific server you configured: `mcp__puppeteer__*` matches every tool from the `puppeteer` server, and `mcp__github__get_*` matches its `get_` tools.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes per-query access control via `allowed_tools` and `disallowed_tools` (allow/deny rules), glob-based matching for MCP tools using `mcp__*` to match all MCP tools across all servers, and server-scoped rules like `mcp__<server>__*` to target a specific MCP server. The page also describes multiple permission modes (default, dontAsk, acceptEdits, bypassPermissions, plan) that control how these rules interact. This substantiates all key aspects of the claim: permissions as MCP tool allow/deny rules, per-query access control, and glob-based rules scoped to all servers (`mcp__*`) or a specific server (`mcp__<server>__`).
